w:r.w:t: Confidential — For Internal and Client Use Only

This Statement of Work (SOW) defines the scope, deliverables, timeline, technical specifications, and commercial terms for the design and development of the official website for Marh Coffee Roasters, a specialty coffee café located in the Hivernage district of Marrakech, Morocco.

Marh Coffee Roasters currently has no proprietary website, and its online presence is limited to third-party platforms including TripAdvisor, Glovo, Yumlo, and Instagram (@marhcoffeeroasters). This project will establish a branded, professional web presence that reflects the quality and identity of the brand, drives foot traffic, and enables online ordering awareness.
Rue Oued Al Makhazine, Hivernage, Marrakech 40000, Morocco

Marh Coffee Roasters is a modern specialty café in the heart of Marrakech's prestigious Hivernage district, positioned opposite the Royal Tennis Club. The brand is dedicated exclusively to 100% Arabica coffee sourced from the highlands of Ethiopia, roasted in small batches to produce balanced and elegant flavour profiles.
Modern, refined aesthetic — appealing to both locals and international travellers
Full café experience: drinks, crêpes, pâtisserie, combos, and retail beans
Award-winning: TripAdvisor Travellers' Choice 2024, ranked #15 of 84 cafés in Marrakech
Active delivery presence on Glovo and Yumlo
The website shall achieve the following business and technical objectives:
Establish an owned, professional web presence that centralises Marh's brand identity
Increase foot traffic to the physical location in Hivernage, Marrakech
Drive online orders via integration links to Glovo and Yumlo
Showcase the full menu with accurate, up-to-date pricing in MAD
Reinforce brand credibility by surfacing TripAdvisor ratings and reviews
Build an audience pipeline through Instagram integration
Provide clear location, hours, and contact information for visitors and travellers
Deliver a single-page or multi-section HTML5 website with no CMS dependency
Implement Three.js-based 3D animations for premium visual impact
Achieve a Lighthouse Performance score of 85+ on desktop
Ensure full mobile responsiveness across all device sizes (320px – 1920px)
Ensure all external links (Glovo, Yumlo, Instagram, TripAdvisor, Google Maps) function correctly
Implement scroll-triggered animations, custom cursor, and micro-interactions
Deploy to a production URL with SSL certificate
The website is a single long-scroll page with distinct named sections, navigable via a fixed top navigation bar. All sections are part of a single HTML file for optimal performance.
Fixed sticky nav: logo, links (Story, Menu, Visit, Order), Book a Table CTA
Full-viewport with Three.js animated background, headline, tagline, CTAs, scroll indicator
Animated scrolling band with brand keywords
Animated counters: 4.7 rating, 27 reviews, 95% Glovo, #15 ranking
Brand narrative, Three.js 3D sphere animation, origin card
Tabbed full menu with 6 categories, item names, descriptions, MAD pricing
Full-width Three.js rotating coffee cup with steam particles
Hours table, 3D animated map, address, directions link
Three testimonial cards, TripAdvisor branding, star ratings
Links to Glovo, Instagram, email with icon buttons
Brand info, navigation, social/platform links, copyright, ratings
The following menu sections and items are included in the website, sourced from Glovo, Yumlo, and TripAdvisor:
Two ristretto pulls, bold and complex
Espresso with silky micro-foam — Ethiopia origin
Classic — espresso, rich foam, warm aromas
Espresso marked with a cloud of foam
Purple yam, steamed milk, espresso — vibrant
Ceremonial grade matcha, oat or whole milk
Spiced chai blend, steamed milk, warm spices
Creamy light vanilla with espresso depth
Ceremonial matcha blended with ice and milk
Purple yam frappé — vibrant and sweet
Melted cheese, smoked ham, golden crêpe
Almond or Lemon Tart + classic coffee
Chocolate chip cookie + frappé of choice
Bright lemon curd in a crisp pastry shell

The website adopts a luxury minimalist aesthetic inspired by specialty coffee culture and Moroccan warmth. The visual language combines editorial typography with dark, earthy tones and hand-crafted 3D visual elements.
22pt / 11px equivalent in docx; 16px body on web
Four distinct Three.js scenes are implemented across the page, each serving a specific narrative and experiential purpose:
Rotating gold torus rings + 80 floating coffee particles. Mouse-reactive camera parallax.
TorusGeometry, SphereGeometry, DirectionalLight, mouse event listeners
Wireframe sphere shell with solid core, orbiting 8 gold dots, ring halo. Represents the origin bean.
WireframeMaterial, TorusGeometry ring, custom orbit animation
Fully modelled 3D coffee cup with saucer, handle, coffee liquid surface, rising steam particles.
CylinderGeometry cup, TorusGeometry saucer edge, steam particle system
Animated top-down city grid with building blocks and glowing pin marker for Hivernage.
BoxGeometry buildings, LineBasicMaterial grid, pulsing pin animation
Custom cursor: 6px solid dot with 32px lagging ring follower
Cursor scale animation on hover of interactive elements
Scroll-triggered reveal animations (fade up + translate Y) with staggered delays
Animated counter for statistics (4.7, 27, etc.) triggered on viewport entry
Sticky navbar with blur/glass effect appearing after 60px scroll
Menu tab switcher with animated gold underline
Review card hover: translate Y -5px + gold border accent
All buttons: translateY(-2px) lift on hover with colour transition
Recommended: Vercel, Netlify, or cPanel hosting
To be added post-launch if required by Client
This document, signed by both parties
Website deployed to agreed hosting with SSL
Up to 2 rounds of revisions included (see §8.4)
The following items are out of scope for the base project but may be added via a separate Change Order:
CMS integration (e.g. Contentful, Sanity) for menu self-management
Custom domain setup and DNS configuration
Google Analytics 4 integration and dashboard setup
SEO optimisation package (meta tags, structured data, sitemap)
Total estimated duration: 4 weeks from signed SOW and receipt of 50% deposit.
SOW signing, deposit invoice, project briefing, content gathering, credential access
Wireframes, visual direction, colour & typography system, component layouts
HTML/CSS build, Three.js 3D integration, menu implementation, animations, mobile responsive
Client review session, feedback incorporation, Round 1 & Round 2 revisions
Browser/device testing, performance audit, link verification, final QA
Domain & hosting setup, deployment, SSL, final handover package
The timeline is contingent on timely Client response. The following inputs must be provided by the Client:
Project does not commence without signature
Minimum 10 high-res photos for hero and sections
Full menu with 6 tabs, real pricing, styling
Hosting setup, SSL, domain config, go-live
Note: All prices are in Moroccan Dirhams (MAD) unless otherwise agreed. A formal quotation will be issued separately.
Due upon delivery of Phase 3 build
Payment accepted via: Bank Transfer (RIB), Western Union, or PayPal
Invoices are due within 5 business days of issuance
Late payment (beyond 10 business days) incurs a 5% monthly surcharge
Work pauses if payment is more than 10 business days overdue
All fees are exclusive of applicable taxes (TVA)

The base project fee includes two (2) rounds of revisions per phase. A revision round is defined as a consolidated set of feedback items submitted via a single communication (email or document). Individual items within one round are not counted separately.
Text corrections, colour adjustments, layout tweaks within the agreed design
Minor menu content updates (item names, prices, descriptions)
Correction of any bugs or errors introduced by the Developer
The following items are considered scope changes and will require a separate Change Order with revised timeline and fee:
Addition of new pages or sections not specified in this SOW
Change of overall design direction after Phase 2 approval
Addition of new technology or integrations (CMS, payment gateway, etc.)
Requests made after final client approval (Phase 5 sign-off)

Upon receipt of full payment, the Client (Marh Coffee Roasters) shall own all intellectual property rights to the final delivered website, including all custom code, layouts, and visual designs created specifically for this project.
Third-party libraries (Three.js, Google Fonts) remain subject to their respective open-source licences (MIT)
Stock photography, if sourced by the Developer, requires Client licensing
The Developer retains the right to display the project in their portfolio unless otherwise requested in writing

The Client warrants that all assets provided (logos, images, text content, trademarks) are owned or licensed by the Client and that the Client has full rights to use them for this purpose. The Developer accepts no liability for Client-provided assets.

Both parties agree to keep all project-related information, pricing, business data, and communications confidential. Neither party shall disclose confidential information to third parties without written consent, except as required by law.

The Developer's total liability under this agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client for this project. The Developer is not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages including lost revenue, lost data, or business interruption.
The Developer is not responsible for third-party platform availability (Glovo, Instagram, TripAdvisor, Google Maps) or changes to external APIs that may affect linked services.
Client pays for work completed. No refund of deposit.
Client pays full fee for completed phases. Partial balance due.
All payments to date refunded. Work delivered up to termination point.
Both parties agree in writing. Fees settled pro-rata for work completed.
Project placed on hold. Reactivation fee may apply.
This SOW is based on the following assumptions. If any assumption proves incorrect, the scope, timeline, or cost may be revised:
The Client will provide all required text content and imagery. Developer will not be responsible for photography.
The menu data (items, descriptions, prices) provided by the Client is accurate and up to date at time of development.
The Client will provide access to domain registrar and hosting control panel for deployment.
No backend database, CMS, or server-side language is required. The website is a static front-end site.
The project is for a publicly accessible, non-e-commerce website. No payment processing is included.
Hosting and domain renewal costs are the Client's responsibility after handover.
TripAdvisor, Glovo, Yumlo, and Instagram pages remain active and publicly accessible.
By signing below, both parties agree to the terms and scope defined in this Statement of Work. This document constitutes a binding agreement upon signature by both parties.
This document was prepared in good faith and reflects the full agreed scope.
Marh Coffee Roasters · Hivernage, Marrakech 40000, Morocco · marhcoffeeroasters@gmail.com
